originalTask:
the MCP doesn't expose container zip upload or website zip upload. The MCP (openapi.json) only has
  listSites, getSite, deploySite, deleteSite, and getEcrCredentials. The openapi-readonly.json is read-only. The
  upload endpoints (/upload, /container/upload, /container/build-dockerfile) exist in the Lambda API but aren't in
   either MCP spec.

add this to the mcp

intents: the MCP doesn't expose container zip upload or website zip u

input.command:
git add mcp/openapi.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add upload, build, and deploy endpoints to MCP OpenAPI spec

The Lambda API had upload, build, and deploy endpoints that weren't
exposed in the MCP spec, preventing AI agents from using them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
---
name: Skip data/Bedrock prompts on container image updates
description: When uploading a new image for an existing container, don't re-ask about EFS data or Bedrock access — those settings were already chosen at container creation
type: feedback
---

When a user uploads a new container image for an existing container, do not ask whether they want EFS persistent data or Bedrock access again.

**Why:** These options were already configured when the container was first created. Re-asking on every image update is redundant and slows down the workflow.

**How to apply:** Only prompt for data/Bedrock settings when creating a brand-new container. On image updates for existing containers, preserve the existing configuration silently.

input.old_string: - [ECR Pull Through Cache](reference_ecr_pull_through_cache.md) — Internal PTC at 891377407345 with cko-pull-through/ prefix for Docker Hub, GHCR, Quay, ECR Public

- [Skip prompts on container updates](feedback_container_upload_skip_prompts.md) — Don't re-ask about data/Bedrock when uploading a new image for an existing container

input.command:
git add dashboard/app.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add build log viewer with copy button; hide bedrock/storage on re-upload

Failed build view now has a "View Build Log" button that fetches and
displays logs with a copy option. Retry/re-upload buttons from both
the static and polling failure views now pass redeploy=true so
schedule, storage, and bedrock options are hidden on container updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.prompt:
I need to understand the MCP OpenAPI specs and Lambda API upload endpoints. Find and read:

1. The MCP OpenAPI spec files (openapi.json and openapi-readonly.json) - full contents
2. The Lambda handler code that implements the upload endpoints: /upload, /container/upload, /container/build-dockerfile
3. Any related request/response schemas or types for these upload endpoints

Look in the `mcp/` and `lambdas/` directories. Be very thorough - read the full contents of all relevant files.
